review_mode: fresh zero-context raw-evidence recount plus coordinator adjudication
source_identity_policy: anonymous logical path plus SHA-256; local absolute paths are intentionally omitted

corrections.finding: The published 6/8 statistic denotes next assistant-response disagreement; parsed next-command disagreement is 4/8.
corrections.resolution: The manuscript and recount ledger now report both units explicitly.

corrections.finding: The legacy 1,000-row citation helper mixed 237 non-document pairs into not_cited and failed on 105 nested-title document pairs.
corrections.resolution: The manuscript uses a balanced-parentheses parser and the 763-row document-only estimand: 61 mentioned/0 real flips and 702 unmentioned/36 real flips, with 35 identity-control flips on the same unmentioned pairs.

corrections.finding: The frozen 4,968 population is all registered non-system block-agent incidences, not a strict downstream-only population.
corrections.resolution: The manuscript relabels the registered unit and reports a downstream sensitivity excluding 1,092 retriever-side cite/document incidences (3,876 remaining).

claims.claim: 18 frozen runs yield 8 cutoff events; all 8 are parent-directory mtime artifacts, 6 response texts differ, 4 next commands differ, 8 suffixes differ, 4 exit statuses differ, and complete next-invocation matches are zero.
evidence: route_a_plus:18 trajectory files enumerated and hashed in descriptive_counts.json

claims.claim: The registered 100-trace population has 4,968 non-system block-agent incidences and 98.09%/95.69% zero-use; the 3,876-incidence downstream sensitivity has 97.70%/94.71%, with median and p90 zero under both definitions.
Among 763 document pairs, 61 title-mentioned pairs have 0 real repair flips and 702 unmentioned pairs have 36; identity control has 35 flips on those same 702 pairs.

The frozen universe contains six prescreened systems, three included audit units, and no qualifying machine-readable runtime instance for AutoGen, LangGraph, or SWE-agent; final levels are Q1/Q0/Q1 and code-only.
On 164 paired code-repair records, reuse/repair damage is 7/164, both-wrong is 58/57, rescues are 2/3, and outcome rejection is 65/164 and 64/164.
All s, rho, break-even, bootstrap, and v=0.01/v=0.10 net-saving values in the manuscript reproduce from the frozen pairs under Delta/C_full.

The proposed testbed has four LLM nodes, no native interactive tool-result consumer or execution bit, 164/164 reconstructed labels, 1,968 condition cells, and an optimistic 3,280-call lower bound, so it is ineligible as proposed.
HumanEvalPack supplies the 164-task Python source/test split; the archived project adds reconstructed pass/fail labels.
evidence: official HumanEvalPack dataset card and OctoPack paper

claims.claim: The anonymous supplement contains allowlisted analysis paths, fixtures, derived ledgers, and manifests, but does not distribute all aggregate raw inputs.

The manuscript reports no new model experiment and Stage 2.5 uses zero studied-system model calls, GPUs, or new trajectories; language-model review agents are part of the disclosed audit process, not the studied system.

remaining_limitations: Complete aggregate reproduction still requires external files at the recorded SHA-256 values.
The 18-run diff slice and the citation/overlap analyses remain post-hoc diagnostics.
The Q rubric has no independent inter-rater validation.
No author identity was supplied to the anonymous review agent, so author-specific self-overlap screening was not performed.
